talk_metadata.talk_title: Spatial transcriptomics technologies and tumor history
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "These measurements are snapshots. You have to kill the sample, or maybe the sample's already dead, when you make the measurement, and they're destructive snapshots."
Speaker said: "We think of it as a kind of a universal platform to move any single-cell measurement, whether that is DNA, RNA, epigenomes, directly into the spatial context."
Speaker said: "We see that the neural crest transitory state is strongly associated with a particular immune suppressive neighborhood..."

key_data_gaps: No functional validation of inferred lineage relationships
No in vivo perturbation data linking spatial niches to therapeutic response

crispro_opportunity.description: Neural crest-like tumor cells are protected by SPP1 and post‑N, suggesting a secreted‑factor mediated resistance that could be targeted.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "When you add SPP1 to cells, it seems to also protect, only in the neural crest cell line state, it protects cells from interferon and TNF cytokine killing."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model secreted factor–receptor networks and predict combinatorial inhibitors to overcome SPP1‑mediated resistance.

crispro_opportunity.description: Spatially distinct tumor cell states (melanocytic vs neural‑crest) correlate with immune niches, offering a spatial biomarker for patient stratification.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Tumor cells in state one are relatively depleted in immune cells in their neighborhood, and then there's a particular enrichment in T cells in tumor cells with state two."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate spatial transcriptomics with outcome data to derive predictive spatial signatures.

cited_competitors.name: Slide‑Seq (Takara)
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as a commercialized version of bar‑coded array technology.

cited_competitors.name: Visium HD
cited_competitors.context: Compared performance metrics (UMIs per cell, genes per cell) against Slide‑Tags.

unresolved_questions: What are the specific antigens recognized by the T‑cell clones associated with tumor sub‑clones?
How can the inferred lineage trees be experimentally validated in human samples?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Decoding tissue architecture in tumors with SEGER and Wormhole
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We don't want to solve this by using a boundary, we're not going to draw a membrane staining, that won't work."
Speaker said: "Our AI is going to understand the phenotypic manifold, cell states, how they relate to each other, not cell types, but the full rich manifold."
Speaker said: "Basal cells are only 4% of the tissue mass, but killing them collapses the entire tumor."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Reliance on CSF2 produced by basal cells to maintain immunosuppressive macrophages

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on nucleus‑centric graph embedding; may be circumvented by alternative segmentation pipelines that improve boundary detection.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Heavy computational demand limits scalability to large clinical cohorts.

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Transformer model trained on specific mouse datasets; may not generalize without extensive retraining to human clinical samples.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Requires massive compute resources; could delay translation to routine pathology.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that basal cells constitute only 4% of tumor mass but concluded that targeting basal cells leads to complete tumor collapse, contradicting the expectation that such a minor population would have limited impact.

key_data_gaps: Lack of human tumor validation for basal‑cell ablation phenotype
No long‑term outcome data after niche disruption

crispro_opportunity.description: CSF2 signaling from basal cells is a vulnerability; CrisPRO can design combinatorial inhibitors to disrupt this niche.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "CSF2 might be doing something here... removing CSF2 just from the basal cells phenocopies collapsing and killing the basal cells."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synergistic targets downstream of CSF2 to mimic basal‑cell ablation without genetic manipulation.

crispro_opportunity.description: SEGER outperforms boundary‑based decontamination methods, offering a more accurate spatial transcriptomics pipeline.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We just say forget the boundary, assign transcripts to cells as link assignment, and that's our big difference."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate SEGER‑processed data to improve downstream computational modeling of spatial niches.

cited_competitors.name: PROSEG
cited_competitors.context: Compared against SEGER, noted to produce 'psychopathically shaped' boundaries.

cited_competitors.name: Visium HD
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as a technology with lower resolution than Slide‑Tags.

unresolved_questions: What are the upstream regulators of CSF2 production in basal cells?
Can basal‑cell ablation be achieved pharmacologically in human PDAC?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Spatial transcriptomes and single‑cell transcriptomes in glioma and head‑and‑neck cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Visium spots are 55 microns and contain 5 to 20 cells, making pattern interpretation challenging."
Speaker said: "In 64% of the spots, one cell state or one cell type controls the vast majority of the cells in a given spot."
Speaker said: "Partial EMT at the tumor core is colocalized with inflammatory myeloid cells and oncostatin M signaling."

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Partial EMT driven by oncostatin M in tumor core
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of therapeutic agents targeting OSM‑OSMR axis in head‑and‑neck cancer

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Spatial heterogeneity of EMT in head‑and‑neck cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No biomarker stratification for EMT pattern (edge vs core) in patient selection
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: EMT spatial pattern (edge vs core) as a predictive biomarker
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential underpowering when subgrouping patients by EMT spatial pattern

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that partial EMT can be induced both by TGF‑β at the tumor edge and by oncostatin M in the core, yet concluded that EMT at the core may have a stronger immunosuppressive effect, despite limited direct functional evidence.

key_data_gaps: Lack of longitudinal clinical outcome linking EMT spatial pattern to therapy response
No functional validation that oncostatin M drives EMT in vivo

crispro_opportunity.description: Targeting OSM‑OSMR signaling together with checkpoint blockade could overcome core‑localized EMT–mediated immunosuppression.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Oncostatin M... induces many of the partial EMT genes" and "EMT at the core is associated with lower lymphocyte infiltration."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate combined OSM inhibition and immunotherapy to predict synergistic effects on tumor microenvironment.

crispro_opportunity.description: Spatial EMT pattern (edge vs core) can serve as a predictive biomarker for patient stratification in trials.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "In some samples, EMT enriched at the edge; in others, EMT enriched at the core... suggests two subtypes of tumors."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate spatial transcriptomics with survival data to develop a spatial EMT signature.

cited_competitors.name: Visium
cited_competitors.context: Described as having 55‑micron spots with mixed cell content.

cited_competitors.name: CODEX / PhenoCycler
cited_competitors.context: Provided single‑cell protein resolution complementary to Visium.

unresolved_questions: What determines whether a tumor adopts edge‑localized versus core‑localized EMT?
Can OSM inhibition reverse core EMT and restore lymphocyte infiltration?

assets_to_track: TGF‑β pathway inhibitors in head‑and‑neck cancer
